fab_section_md:
- King of the Seven Kingdoms under his mother's regency; he wed his sister Alysanne in a sudden secret marriage that took the Hand and Queen Regent unawares (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:35)
- He remained on Dragonstone, unwilling to be ruled by Rogar Baratheon though grateful for his help to the throne (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:99)
- He expected to be tested as his father had been (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:107)
identity_line: Jaehaerys I Targaryen — King of the Seven Kingdoms under his mother's regency; he wed his sister Alysanne in a sudden secret marriage that took the Hand and Queen Regent unawares.

fab_section_md:
- Fifthborn child of Aenys and Alyssa, a maid of thirteen who pledged her troth to her brother against her mother's wishes (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:27)
- Remembered as a bright, biddable child without her sister's timidity (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:115)
- She was in no haste to leave Dragonstone and welcomed the arriving Wise Women with gifts (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:221)
identity_line: Alysanne Targaryen — Fifthborn child of Aenys and Alyssa, a maid of thirteen who pledged her troth to her brother against her mother's wishes.

fab_section_md:
- Queen Regent, widow of King Aenys, wife of the Hand Rogar Baratheon, and mother of the three queens' generation (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:27)
- She wished the king's rash marriage undone, fearing it would set the Faith against the Crown, yet could seldom bring herself to act; as Septon Barth wrote, she "desired above all to be loved" (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:69)
- She saw the sending of companions as a chance to undo the marriage (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:143)
identity_line: Alyssa Velaryon — Queen Regent, widow of King Aenys, wife of the Hand Rogar Baratheon, and mother of the three queens' generation.

fab_section_md:
- Hand of the King and husband of the Queen Regent; humiliated when forced to back down at the gates of Dragonstone (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:53)
- His first impulse was to take Dragonstone by storm until Alyssa dissuaded him (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:57)
- He wanted eyes and ears on Dragonstone and the marriage's consummation prevented (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:151)
- Septon Barth's charity is invoked for him (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:55)
identity_line: Rogar Baratheon — Hand of the King and husband of the Queen Regent; humiliated when forced to back down at the gates of Dragonstone.

fab_section_md:
- Younger daughter of Morgan Wylde of the Rain House; seduced at thirteen by a stable groom and confined to bear a bastard son in 48 AC (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:175)
- She was among the companions sent to Dragonstone and is the alleged author of A Caution for Young Girls (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:165)
- According to that book, a great lord met her at an inn and charged her to beguile the king into bed, though the man's identity is disputed (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:183)
identity_line: Coryanne Wylde — Younger daughter of Morgan Wylde of the Rain House; seduced at thirteen by a stable groom and confined to bear a bastard son in 48 AC.

fab_section_md:
- Second son of Lord Farman, nine years younger than Rhaena, mocked even at his father's court as "half a girl" and never made a knight (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:231)
- Rhaena said she chose him for his kindness (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:233)
identity_line: Androw Farman — Second son of Lord Farman, nine years younger than Rhaena, mocked even at his father's court as "half a girl" and never made a knight.

fab_section_md:
- Androw's elder sister, three years his senior, sharp of wit and tongue, whose great love was sailing and who longed to sail beyond the Sunset Sea (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:241)
- Lord Farman's maester wrote that Rhaena's true love on Fair Isle was Elissa, not Androw (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:239)
identity_line: Elissa Farman — Androw's elder sister, three years his senior, sharp of wit and tongue, whose great love was sailing and who longed to sail beyond the Sunset Sea.

fab_section_md:
- Maegor the Cruel, uncle of Jaehaerys and Rhaena, brother of Aenys, son of Visenya, who returned from across the narrow sea to seize the Iron Throne (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:129)
- He made Jaehaerys and Alysanne wards of Visenya and demanded Rhaena's capture (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:235)
identity_line: Maegor I Targaryen — Maegor the Cruel, uncle of Jaehaerys and Rhaena, brother of Aenys, son of Visenya, who returned from across the narrow sea to seize the Iron Throne.

fab_section_md:
- King, first husband of Alyssa and father of Jaehaerys, slighted as weak beside his warrior brother Maegor (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:103)
- He died on Dragonstone, after which Maegor seized the throne (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:129)
identity_line: Aenys I Targaryen — King, first husband of Alyssa and father of Jaehaerys, slighted as weak beside his warrior brother Maegor.

fab_section_md: - Commander of the Dragonstone castle garrison and father of Ser Alyn and Ser Howard (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:103)
identity_line: Merrell Bullock — Commander of the Dragonstone castle garrison and father of Ser Alyn and Ser Howard.

fab_section_md: - Seasoned master-at-arms on Dragonstone who trained the king and judged he might best a risen Maegor (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:103)
identity_line: Elyas Scales — Seasoned master-at-arms on Dragonstone who trained the king and judged he might best a risen Maegor.

fab_section_md: - Aged Maester of Dragonstone, eighty and serving since Visenya's day (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:79)
identity_line: Culiper — Aged Maester of Dragonstone, eighty and serving since Visenya's day.

fab_section_md: - Septon Barth, called the wisest man ever to serve as Hand, quoted on human nature and on Queen Alyssa's yearnings (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:17)
identity_line: Barth — Septon Barth, called the wisest man ever to serve as Hand, quoted on human nature and on Queen Alyssa's yearnings.

fab_section_md: - Septon Mattheus, who spoke of little but how decent folk would condemn the king's incestuous union (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:59)
identity_line: Mattheus — Septon Mattheus, who spoke of little but how decent folk would condemn the king's incestuous union.

fab_section_md: - Fiercely pious wife of the Lord of Riverrun, set over Alysanne's maids and charged by Rogar to report on the king and queen (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:145)
identity_line: Lucinda Tully — Fiercely pious wife of the Lord of Riverrun, set over Alysanne's maids and charged by Rogar to report on the king and queen.

fab_section_md: - Lucinda's younger sister, a modest maid whose name had briefly been offered as a match for Jaehaerys (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:145)
identity_line: Ella Broome — Lucinda's younger sister, a modest maid whose name had briefly been offered as a match for Jaehaerys.

fab_section_md: - Eldest of Lord Rogar's four brothers, named in some versions of the book as the man at the inn and later blamed by Archmaester Crey for the calumny (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:193)
identity_line: Borys Baratheon — Eldest of Lord Rogar's four brothers, named in some versions of the book as the man at the inn and later blamed by Archmaester Crey for the calumny.

fab_section_md: - Lord of the Rain House and father of Coryanne (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:211)
identity_line: Morgan Wylde — Lord of the Rain House and father of Coryanne.

fab_section_md: - Archmaester writing late in Jaehaerys's reign, who held the inn meeting a calumny meant to blacken Lord Rogar (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:207)
identity_line: Crey — Archmaester writing late in Jaehaerys's reign, who held the inn meeting a calumny meant to blacken Lord Rogar.

fab_section_md: - Maester and the Citadel's foremost expert on obscene texts, who dismissed the tale as a bawdy invention (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:207)
identity_line: Ryben — Maester and the Citadel's foremost expert on obscene texts, who dismissed the tale as a bawdy invention.

fab_section_md: - Maester serving Lord Farman, who wrote to the Citadel that Rhaena found her true love in Elissa (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:237)
identity_line: Smike — Maester serving Lord Farman, who wrote to the Citadel that Rhaena found her true love in Elissa.

fab_section_md: - Grand Maester who attributes the plan of sending companions to Queen Alyssa (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:143)
identity_line: Benifer — Grand Maester who attributes the plan of sending companions to Queen Alyssa.

fab_section_md: - Aged Septon who kept the sept on Dragonstone and performed the royal wedding (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:145)
identity_line: Oswyck — Aged Septon who kept the sept on Dragonstone and performed the royal wedding.

fab_section_md: - Formidable Septa sent to see to Alysanne's religious instruction (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:145)
identity_line: Ysabel — Formidable Septa sent to see to Alysanne's religious instruction.

fab_section_md: - Rhaena's willful and stubborn daughter (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:117)
identity_line: Aerea Targaryen — Rhaena's willful and stubborn daughter.

fab_section_md: - Rhaena's late first husband, Prince Aegon, who faced Maegor at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye (fab-surfeit-of-rulers-08-p01:27)
identity_line: Aegon the Uncrowned — Rhaena's late first husband, Prince Aegon, who faced Maegor at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye.
